description: Implement the public-facing pages: Home page with hero section and album list, and album detail page with Pinterest-style masonry grid.
steps: Create src/components/HeroSection.tsx with full-width gradient hero
Create src/components/MasonryGrid.tsx with CSS columns layout
Create src/components/AlbumCard.tsx with image, name, photo count, and hover effects
Update src/app/page.tsx as Server Component fetching albums from Supabase
Create src/app/[slug]/page.tsx for album detail with masonry photo grid
Update src/app/layout.tsx with navbar and dark theme
Update src/app/globals.css with dark theme variables and system fonts
Update next.config.ts with remotePatterns for supabase.co images
Verify build passes with unset NODE_OPTIONS && npm run build
acceptance_criteria: Hero section displays 'Welcome to Dump' with gradient background
Home page fetches and displays albums from Supabase in masonry-style grid
Album cards show cover image, name, photo count with hover effects
Clicking album card navigates to /[slug] album detail page
Album detail page shows photos in Pinterest-style masonry grid
Album detail handles not found case with notFound()
Layout has sticky navbar with 'Dump' linked to home
Next.js Image configured for Supabase remote patterns
findings: 1. Next.js 16 uses Promise-based params in dynamic route pages (params: Promise<{ slug: string }>) requiring await. 2. Used 'unoptimized' prop on Next.js Image in AlbumCard to avoid image optimization issues with dynamic Supabase URLs. 3. Used regular img tags in album detail for photos since they have varied natural heights ideal for masonry. 4. The home page route is static (prerendered) while [slug] is dynamic (server-rendered on demand). 5. Supabase aggregation query with photos(count) returns data in format album.photos[0].count.
